onboarding.welcomeSubtitle: Securely watch a computer from a distance — or let someone watch yours. We'll set this computer up. You only need to do this once.
onboarding.roleQuestion: What will you use this computer for?
onboarding.roleHint: Pick what fits. You can choose both.
onboarding.hostTitle: I want to get help on this computer
onboarding.hostSubtitle: Someone else can then watch this screen — but only when you click “Yes” at that moment. Choose this if you want to be helped.
onboarding.clientTitle: I want to help someone else
onboarding.clientSubtitle: You watch someone else's computer to help them. Choose this if you're the one helping.
onboarding.changeLater: You can always change this later.
onboarding.serverTitle: Which server do you connect to?
onboarding.serverSubtitle: RivetLink uses a server as a go-between to make the connection. You need its address — usually you get it from your administrator, or you run your own server.
onboarding.nameHint: A name you recognise, for example “Work”.
onboarding.serverHint: The web address of the server. The app takes care of the rest.
onboarding.deviceSubtitle: This is how others see which computer is yours.

connect.noRelayBefore: No active server yet. Add one under
connect.signInSubtitle: Sign in to see the computers in your organisation.
connect.noDevices: No computers in your organisation yet.
connect.requestingSession: Requesting a session — the host may ask for approval…
connect.sessionCodeSubtitle: Ad-hoc support without an account — the host shares a code.
connect.sessionCodeNote: This connection method is still being built (server + protocol).

device.identitySubtitle: Give this key to a support client so they can trust it in advance (TOFU).
device.hostAgentIntro: To make this PC truly controllable, a host agent will run that:
device.hostConsent: Asks for approval on every connection (consent dialog)
device.hostScreen: Shares the screen and receives input
device.hostAgentNote: This integration (rivetlink-agent) is the next milestone.

relays.title: Saved servers
relays.name: Name
relays.serverHint: The app creates the secure connection itself.

settings.name: Name
settings.privateNote: The private key never leaves this device.
